rationale: ADR fitness: all four cited ADRs resolve to real files under .mindspec/adr/. ADR-0037 carries exactly 5 prior dated amendments, so the §1/§3/§7 + §6/§8 amendment follows its established convention; §3 is correctly framed as a genuine extension (matching the 109/ADR-0040 precedent), and the §7 durable-vs-best-effort distinction is justified by 'refutation changes the gate outcome.' The empty-reason parity claim is GROUNDED: abandon leg (3) at gate.go:163-166 clears (Warn) even with an empty abandon_reason, so the §8 re-affirmation is truthful, not invented. ADR-0035 recovery wiring verified at panel_advisory.go:201-203 (guard.NewFailure); HasFinalRecoveryLine + recovery_convention_test.go exist. ADR-0040 floor stays single-homed (panel.go:113-127, leg 10 byte-preserved). ADR-0023 audit-on-metadata reasoning sound. Testing strategy: every load-bearing case names real, runnable infra. AC11's completeMergeMetadataFn is a swappable package var (complete.go:42); readStubMergeExecutor.completeCalled real; step-4.75 placement before CompleteBead (complete.go:669) makes 'merge never ran' assertable. All named fixtures verified present. No un-runnable or faked test.
findings: VERIFIED (ADR): ADR-0037/0035/0040/0023 all resolve to real files; ADR-0037 has 5 prior dated amendments so the amendment convention is honored. §3 correctly framed as extension, §7 durable-audit distinction coherent with the ADR model.
VERIFIED (ADR): empty-reason footgun parity is real — gate.go:163-166 shows empty abandon_reason still clears (Warn), so the §8 re-affirmation of empty-refutation-reason parity is truthful.

VERIFIED (ADR-0035): leg-Block routes through guard.NewFailure with a genuine recovery line (panel_advisory.go:201-203); guard.HasFinalRecoveryLine + internal/guard/recovery_convention_test.go exist, so the new leg-9.5 and step-4.75 failures conform by construction with no ADR-0035 amendment needed.

VERIFIED (testing/AC11): completeMergeMetadataFn is a package-level swappable seam (complete.go:42); the durable write at step 4.75 precedes exec.CompleteBead (complete.go:669), and readStubMergeExecutor.completeCalled (panel_gate_e2e_test.go) makes the strong 'merge never ran' assertion real and runnable.

VERIFIED (testing/AC7-9): decision-table rows build on synthetic *Result fixtures via result()/regn() (panel_decision_test.go:13-44) and makeVerdicts (votedecision_test.go:94); Result.Verdicts is exposed so UnresolvedVerdicts()/AppliedRefutations() are testable. The result() helper today synthesizes only APPROVE/REJECT, but the plan explicitly names upgrading it to emit RC/unrecognized strings + refutations — acknowledged, not a gap.
VERIFIED (testing/AC10): fixed-string no-advertise predicate mirrors the real TestPanelGate_BlockNeverNamesSkipVar (panel_gate_e2e_test.go:290).

VERIFIED (shared infra): setupPanelGateRepo, approveVerdicts, subThresholdVerdicts, writePanel, TestWritePanelAuditMetadata_Abandoned, buildResult, beadPanel/sixApproves all exist as named — no forked or invented harness.

MINOR (non-blocking, testing): the AC2 e2e passing-by-refutation on setupPanelGateRepo will need completeMergeMetadataFn stubbed to a capturing success (the durable write returns its error, and real bd metadata may not be wired in the temp-repo harness). The plan's 'captures the merged map' language implies this stub; the seam is designed for it, so no change required — flagging only so the fixer installs the capturing stub rather than relying on real bd.MergeMetadata.
